Title: J065--Sterile Cabinets PMI
SetASide: Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business Set Aside
PopStreetAddress: Department of Veterans Affairs NCO 22 - Network Contracting 3601 S. 6th Avenue
Description: The West Los Angeles VA Medical Center was seeking a vendor that could provide services related to a Preventative Maintenance and Inspection (PMI) program for sterile storage cabinets used in clinical areas that have issues with temperature and humidity. The Contractor is to provide all necessary labor, materials, supplies, parts, software (needed for the general operation of the equipment), tools, equipment, travel, transportation expenses and other related costs to provide services related to a Preventative Maintenance and Inspection (PMI) program for sterile storage cabinets used in clinical areas that have issues with temperature and humidity. The Contractor is to perform work in strict accordance with the specifications, statement of work, and all VA local, federal, state, and applicable regulations. All parts will be Original equipment manufacturer (OEM). Contractor is responsible for all subcontractors required to complete reports. All proposals were to be sent to the Network Contracting Office (NCO) 22 Contract Specialist, Jamie ElBedawi at Jamie.elbedawi@va.gov. Award was based upon a comparative evaluation of quotes in accordance with the Simplified Acquisition Procedures of FAR 13. Comparative evaluation is the side by side pairwise comparison of quotes based on factors resulting in a Contracting Officer decision for the quote most favorable to the Government. The award was made to the response most advantageous to the Government. Submission of your response were to be received not later than 17:00 MST on 02/20/2026 at Jamie.elbedawi@va.gov. ALL communication were to be in writing ONLY, to this same email address. Late submissions were treated in accordance with the solicitation provision at FAR 52.212-1(f).

Title: Portable Latrine Services - Devens RFTA, MA
Awardee: LOUIS UNLIMITED PROSPERITY, LLC Cambridge MA 02140 USA
Description: On behalf of the Devens Reserve Forces Training Area (RFTA), the U.S. Army Contracting Command – New Jersey, Warfighter Support Division has awarded Contract W15QKN-26-P-A013 to procure portable latrine services for the DRFTA in Devens, MA. The requirement is for portable chemical latrine units, including permanent emplacements (51), temporary units (up to 600 per contract year), and associated servicing/cleaning. The Performance Work Statement (PWS) defines the requirements for the rental, delivery, placement, maintenance, and cleaning of portable chemical latrines at the DRFTA, Massachusetts. The resulting award will be a fixed-price contract and will consist of one (1) 12-month Base-Period and four (4) 12-month Option-Periods. This requirement was issued as a 100% total small business set-aside and was awarded on 20 March 2026 to Louis Unlimited Prosperity, LLC of Cambridge, MA for the total amount (Base-Year plus four Option-Years) of $349,759.80. Please see the attached Award Notice.

Title: REPAIR SERVICES FOR THE SCS PROGRAM
Awardee: UNITED STATES TECHNOLOGIES INC Fair Lawn NJ 07410 USA

Title: Bomber Supplemental Trainer for Aircrew Readiness (BSTAR) Production Other Transaction SBIR Phase III
Description: The BSTAR program is designed to enhance the proficiency of bomber aircrew by integrating innovative training solutions, implementing comprehensive training curricula, and addressing unique operational challenges within the B-1 bomber community. It is intended to enhance and sustain foundational airmanship, instrument proficiency, and crew coordination skills outside the primary Mission Design Series (MDS), using a high-tempo, low-cost supplemental trainer. Performance data, training outcomes, and airmanship development trends from this effort will inform decisions regarding sustainment, scaling, and potential enterprise implementation of a steady-state BSTAR Companion Trainer Program. This initiative aligns with USAF strategic objectives to improve B-1 aircrew operational efficiency across its bomber fleet. This program will aim to aid the B-1 aviators through supplemental training throughout the aircrew members’ careers within AFGSC.
